review_text: Had such an amazing stay here. The staff were incredible, and to top it off we were unexpectedly upgraded which made the whole trip even more special. The park itself is beautiful, peaceful, and in such a perfect location with stunning views. Everything felt easy, comfortable, and well looked after. You can really tell the team cares about creating a great experience for guests. We left feeling so happy and refreshed — definitely one of the highlights of our trip!
Deluxe site gave more space

Me and my family would like to say THANK YOU to @discovery Park at Jindabyne ❤️ the staff was so friendly👍easy to check in 🫶🏼 and OMG the room is neat, clean and it so cosy❤️ we feel like home when we stay for the Anzac long weekend with you!!! The location is close to the shop and is easily to get to Thredbo for my family playing Alpine Coaster and exploring so much!!!
I will surely recommend everyone should stay @Discovery Park and YES mẹ and my Family we will always coming back to Discovery Park ❤️🫶🏼🍂👍
Clean, well-ordered and good facilities.

Really enjoyed my stay here. We stayed in the deluxe cabin, close to the lake. The cabin was super clean and spacious. Enjoyed sitting on the deck with a cup of tea and strolling around to the lakeside. Also liked the sms service, handy for alerts. The spa was huge and great for relaxing in at the end of the day. Coffee, cycling and kayaking are all within walking distance. And service was friendly. Great relaxing spot, would go again.

We enjoyed our stay at the park. We like the nice chilled vibe, beautiful autumn trees, and scenic lake view. We also liked the wood fire. Bike riding and kayaking around the lake is so fun. A comment to improve the place : reception ran out of toilet paper and made no effort to give us more.
Nice cabins on the lake and  it to far to access the mountains
Heated bathrooms, beautiful vista. Lovely park
Amazing staff - friendly, helpful, accomodating and informative. The cabins, amenities and entire park was awesome. Thanks Anna and Crystal for everything that made our holiday perfect!
Fantastic place everything you could want in accommodations. Really clean and very comfortable. Within easy distance to Threadbow and Perisher
Miejsce spokojne w przepięknej okolicy nad jeziorem.
Nice park a bit out of town

Just checked into this park unannounced. What a wonderful welcome by 4 friendly staff. Great site overlooking Lake Jindabyne and only $7 a night (unpowered, $22 for powered)|Amenities include, showers, toilets, camp kitchen, TV room, sauna and spa, laundry, jumping pillow and games.
Nice park & good amenities. Located on the lale

I love love love staying here - I’ve done two weeks in winter and now two weeks in summer. Firstly, the staff are amazing. The people who work the front desk are so helpful, kind and genuinely make me want to go and just have a chat with them. The facilities and amenities are great. I’ve stayed in most cabin types now and I think they’re all fantastic. It’s clean,  well-maintained and has everything you could need. I honestly prefer staying here then on mountain. I can’t recommend enough and again the staff are just incredible. Did I mention Enough how great the staff are?

Stayed here in summer, non powered camping site. Dusty, no grass but otherwise clean and tidy. Pleasant breeze and view over the lake.Amenities a bit tired, but very clean, excellent communual kitchen including ovens.About 30 minutes drive to Thredbo.
It is a fantastic place by the lake Zindabyne for a outing. Loved our stay here
Clean, complete kitchen with appliances. Just what you need when living away from home.

Vi bookede en enkelt overnatning hos Discovery Parks, fin pris og venligt personale ved ankomst, men campsite lå mest af alt på noget der mindede om en parkeringsplads. Ingen græs, bare fliser og direkte op ad både nabo og genbo.
Spa / sauna faciliteterne er beskidte og gamle, så trænger virkelig til en kærlighånd og en rengøring. Bruser og toiletter er fine, middelstandard. Placeringen er ude i ingenting og midt på en afkørsel, så hvis man ønsker lidt liv og by, skal man ikke vælge denne campsite.
Hyggeligt at campsite ligger lige ned til søen.
Lots of bugs and broken vents in toilet
Amazing place. Facilities were clean and well maintained. Close to the lake and bike paths. Easy access to town, trout hatchery and only 30mins drive to hikes/biking. Excellent value for money!

I enjoyed my stay. Cabins are of acceptable quality and clean. Well, you can't expect the experience of a hotel though. The rooms and beds are small but fine. There's basic facilities such as laundry, bbq, games room, etc.

Well set-up and conveniently located park for a jump off to Thredbo, or walk out of your caravan, tent or bungalow and onto Lake Jindabyne.
It’s also right on the paved shared path from town along the lakeshore.
All of the expected amenities, clean and expansive bathroom, camp kitchen, lounge with fireplace, games room and massive laundry.
Only real downside is that the unpowered campsites have no grass. Bare as a billiard ball. Don’t forget your drill if you need to drive pegs!
We stayed 5 nights in a Standard 2-bed cabin. Very small bedrooms - we had 3 adults and a 5 year old and it was very tight. Facilities in-cabin were good though.

We have stayed here before & if it hadn't been for a last minute encounter with the receptionist I wouldn't hesitate to give it a 4 or 5 star. We asked if we can leave our 3 cabin size bags somewhere so we can take kids for last day skiing but receptionist claimed there is no space in office which obviously there wasn't but plenty of space elsewhere in the building which we would have been happy to leave at own risk. We ended up driving back & no skiing for kids.
Suggestion to management is to watch older staff so they don't take their jobs for granted & understand if customers are asking for something reasonable they should make an effort to help instead of treat guests like fools..

War in einer Cabine mit einem Elternschlafzimmer und einem Kinderzimmer mit zwei Stockbetten (4 Schlafmöglichkeiten). Es gibt unterschiedlich ausgestattete Cabins für mehr oder weniger Personen. Mir war wichtig, dass es eine kleine Terrasse mit Sitzmöglichkeiten und Blick auf dem See gibt. Die Cabine ist funktional und etwas knapp ausgestattet. In der Küche hätte ich mir wenigstens ein Schneidbrett gewünscht. Ansonsten ist alles da was man für einen Aufenthalt benötigt. Die Ausstattung ist nicht neu aber es funktioniert alles. Park befinden sich direkt an der Cabine. Das Personal ist freundlich und hilfsbereit.

Great and friendly staff and the accommodation were the best that I've been in. The location and surrounds were absolutely brilliant when you see the sunrise over the lake. Weather can be hit or miss but when a hit its brilliant.
Unpowered site amenities clean and warm.

Had an amazing stay! We got an end of season online sale that enabled us to spent a couple of days at Perisher 😍 The staff were super friendly and helpful and the sauna and spa where amazing after a day on the slopes. I only wish they had later run times or kid free times gets quite busy. Thank you 🙏🏼 I’ll be Back! 🤖

Staff were amazing, me and my friend got upgraded to a newer cabin. Amenities and everything were great the spa and sauna after the snow. Highly recommend this place the staff were very kind and accommodating perfect holiday away. Highly recommend.

I stayed here from September school holidays for a week with three young kids.  The staff when I booked were helpful.  They upgraded me to a spa two bedroom from a two bedroom cabin for free. We got there late for check in and found the cabin easily.  We did not have any problems with the place. Ample space.  There is a laundry $4 each load,  dryer $4 each time,  games room,  TV room with DVDs, tennis court,  play ground,  right in front of the lake,  kitchen with four stove s, fridges to share,  a lounge room with fireplace,  showers and toilets,  chess set and bouncing pit. The kids loved it. We would come again.
Excellent amenities well cared for and clean
Great park with excellent, very clean amenities and very friendly/helpful staff!

Very nice caravan and cabin park, friendly helpful staff, reasonable prices and well maintained park. Staying here in Jindabyne is the most cost-effective way for day trip to the snowfields.
Have stayed here a few times over the years and always had a good experience. Recommended.

I made a booking with this hotel but cancelled it by receiving a credit for about $872. I didn't pay attention to the expiry date. When I tried to use the credit today, I was told that it would expire after 1 year. Just now when I mentioned this to a friend, she said according to Australian Consumer Law, they should give me at least 3 years time to use the credit. It is not a small amount. I feel this business is trying to take advantedge of its clinets. I am going to make a complaint to ACCC.

Fantastic spot for a campervan adventure with friends! 🚐👫 The views are stunning and the atmosphere is peaceful and quiet - perfect for unwinding. The BBQ spot is a great touch, too! 🍔🔥 Loved every moment of our stay. Highly recommend for a relaxing getaway with nature. 🌳👌 5 stars! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️"

On arrival staff were not welcoming, stayed in a 2 x bedroom cabin very small & expensive. Bad smell, oven, microwave & kitchen exhaust were filthy to the point we couldn't use them. Tried to use oven smoked out the whole cabin due to fat never being cleaned properly, called management young man came with windex & cloth to clean oven out which made no difference ended up throwing our $40 dinner in the bin due to not being able to use the oven. Toilet brush holder full of dirty soiled water, games room air hocky machine takes money but does not have any pucks to even play. Good location and heating. Thats about it.

Stayed four nights in a superior cabin with fireplace.
The cabin itself was excellent: new furnishings and fairly new with full kitchen and bathroom. The heating with the fireplace reverse cycle AC and main bedroom panel heater were fantastic to keep us warm.
The amenities are also very good with a room to adjust skis/snowboards, drying room (which we didn’t have a need for) and the kitchen/laundry/games room.
Didn’t visit reception as we arrived late and were out at the snow for all reception hours for the remainder of our stay, however we had a split booking and prior to our arrival I called to see if they could be merged so we wouldn’t need to move and they were extremely accommodating!
Such a great location close to the turnoff for both Perisher and thredbo, with a petrol station, good Thai restaurant and cafe attached for extra convenience.

If you’re looking for a family vibe or quiet stay away from the noise and traffic of central Jindy, this is definitely it! Will be back next season.
We enjoyed our stay in the 2 bedroom cabins with a fireplace. Beautiful facilities heaps of room and right at the base of thredbo !

on the middle of the way with my family to Jimdabyne, they called us and decided to cancel the booking we made weeks ago and said we couldn’t stay more on the parking because there wasn’t spots available for our motorhome, we were literally 1h away to arrive there and had to cancel the trip because there wasn’t spots available any where, really bad service.

Our winter stay at Discovery Parks Jindabyne in a standard cabin was quite pleasant. The accommodation was comfortable and met our basic needs without any major issues. The cabin was clean and adequately heated, which made it cozy during the chilly nights. The location is very accessible, conveniently close to local shops and ski resorts, making it ideal for winter activities. While the cabin is simple and not luxurious, it offers great value for money. Overall, it’s a good choice for travelers seeking a practical and well-located base for their winter adventures in Jindabyne.

I took my niece and nephew to see the snow for the first time. We were greeted by two lovely ladies on the 24th of June and were kindly given playing cards and asked to take as much dvds to watch. I did notice that I had booked a small cabin, however upon arrival.. I swear we were UPGRADED! Lol so that gave us so much joy. Our cabin location was perfect, we had the view of the river and mountains with snow on top! As we entered the cabin, it was super duper cosy as they left the heater on for us. The whole cabin was so cute and we basically felt like home! The shower was soooooo nice and warm with those heat lights. We will be booking again for next year and many years to come! This was the best snow trip I could have ask for. 100 out of 10!!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had a wonderful stay at Discovery Parks - Jindabyne! The cabin was cozy, clean, and perfect for our little winter escape. My kids absolutely loved it — it was warm, welcoming, and very family-friendly. The atmosphere around the park was peaceful and friendly, and everything felt well taken care of. It’s a great spot to relax, especially with little ones. We’ll definitely be back. Thank you for a lovely experience! 💕

First of all i love the stay and the team is friendly nice.. a night for $144 is worth it.. the room is look excellent clean and exactly same as showing on ads.. love it.. how ever i am giving 2 stars just because i got annoyed about WiFi.. i stayed at house number 14 and it is absolutely no wifi signal at this room.. the phone reception is bad and i was hoping wifi would help me out for the internet and whatever.. i totally love the place but not love the wifi :(
Great all round. Very kid friendly

I made a booking online by mistake and immediately emailed explaining this. The staff I spoke to on the phone the next day were friendly and polite but did not have the authority to grant a refund. I asked to speak with someone in management and was told they would call me.
No one called me, rather they left it in the notes for staff to tell me I could not have even a partial refund.

I would understand them keeping some portion for the admin I even offered 25% but they wouldn’t negotiate. Keeping $866 on what was obviously a genuine mistake is very rude. (And Im wondering if even illegal??)
I’m quite shocked by the way I have been treated.

Think twice before booking here !

There is an NRMA caravan park down the road it’s much cheaper and better!! Stay there!|Have a look at the google reviews. The management at the NRMA park respond personally to everyone. Discovery park treat customers like they don’t exist.||I made a booking online by mistake and immediately emailed explaining this. The staff I spoke to on the phone the next day were friendly and polite but did not have the authority to grant a refund. I asked to speak with someone in management and was told they would call me.|No one called me, rather they left it in the notes for staff to tell me I could not have even a partial refund.|I would understand them keeping some portion for the admin I even offered 25% but they wouldn’t negotiate. Keeping $866 on what was obviously a genuine mistake is very rude. (And Im wondering if even illegal??)|I’m quite shocked by the way I have been treated.|Think twice before booking here !

Stayed at the Jindabyne Discovery Park for for a week for my partner’s work training. The park is in an excellent location by the lake, has great shared facilities including a sauna, and a variety of cabins to suit your needs.

The park had some unexpected maintenance required which affected the power and water for a period of our stay. After our stay I reached out to the park. Promptly and without hesitation they offered a credit towards my next stay. This was excellent service and I look forward to visiting again soon.

Thank you to Gerry and the team.

Booked our stay on a premium powered site which is 25ft, paid in full. Got the booking confirmation, all good. Got an additional email asking us the size of our van, which i had put the details in original booking, which is not huge at 19.6, advised car length and confirmed we were not brining any other equipment. 2 hours later a call from reception cancelling our booking as our van is too big and we won't fit or be able to reverse on the site. Then asked receptionist how do we get a refund of our money paid, we don't have a payment. Yes you do, we have the confirmation that says so. There are a lot more vans much bigger than us. Surely this is not correct. So disappointed.

We stayed 2 weeks here in Cabin 50. This is situated right under the main road so we were, unfortunately, treated to heavy truck traffic and exhaust breaks from 6:00 am each morning. No hint of a view of the lake. DVD player was broken. We reported this and were told that there was no replacement as the DVD players were being phased out. Kitchen was so tiny, we kept accidentally bumping on the gas cook cooktop while trying to move around each other. No microwave dishes available to cook or heat anything in otherwise kitchen appliances were adequate. Water pressure was appalling in the shower and equated more to a trickle. We were often cold and found the air conditioning only really heated the main area. It snowed while we were there so a bit more warmth would have been appreciated. There is a great amenities block within the park with a large laundry, tv room, games room and fire place. We would not choose to stay in the same cabin again but, judging by other reviews, requests do not appear to be guaranteed.

We arrived late, however reception had messaged us with details on how to check in. We found our cabin, opened up and it was warm, the staff had turned the heater on for us knowing we were going to arrive late.

Our cabin was clean and modern. Spacious enough for the 3 of us. Staff was amazing, customer service exceptional. Trader Joe's next door had great coffee and food. There's a Shell service station and a Thai Restaurant. Town is a 5min drive away.

We enjoyed our time spent at Jindabyne that we're looking at booking another short break at Discovery Parks, Jindabyne.

Upon arriving at reception I knew that our stay here would be great.Reception staff were friendly and obliging.Our cabin was well equipped with everything needed for  an extended stay. The kids camped. The park is well laid out with plenty of amenities.

Cabin was good..enjoyed the location but 3 stars simply because the cabin really needed a good clean especially the lounge chairs ..rubbish etc looked like it had been there for a long time..young backpackers on front desk really had no idea about much of what was on offer and seemed to not understand the most basic questions ..wouldnt stay again due to these problems

It was okay, sites are tight depending on where they place you but I don't think they have any clue. Super friendly until you have a concern or query, managed to obtain a refund on my 'extended stay'. Not likely to stay there again, location is inconvenient and there doesn't seem to be a care factor re: customer satisfaction. I moved to the NRMA Park and ended up staying a month, a lot more professionally run and great staff there.
Always a pleasant experience.
Nice park. Friendly staff. Nice camp kitchen but needs more utensils. Showers took a long time to get warm

The deluxe caravan sites are a bit tricky to reverse into as trees and poles are very close to the edge of the lane making it difficult. These sites also come a raised picnic area but it was too hot to use. A wooden table is provided but it had no hole for an umbrella ( which we had) most people sat in the shade of their vans. Maybe plant some trees to help shade them. The views are amazing. The staff are wonderful and friendly. The water pressure is average in the showers. Just a couple of things could improve but overall a lovely spot.

Staff are very friendly, rooms are clean and tidy, trader Joe's cafe attached at the front is perfect for a morning coffee, the lake is great for a swim after a big day riding, I will be back for sure.
The best price at the moment I can see anywhere on any app or site in NSW or Victoria

Best holiday park cabin I have ever stayed. Double Glazed window with fire place. The room is very modern and bathroom is new too!! I am so happy to stay there for couple of nights during my hiking trip.

Stayed here a few years ago, great price outside of school holidays (we paid $88, then it jumped to $220 a night and we were winging it so went home).
The rooms were basic but cosy and for the price, great value. Enjoyable communal areas.  Good access to the snowys, located after all the roundabouts! But still close to town.

Good place, excellent cabins.
Only two drawbacks; the cabins are rather squashed together - you really need to book one of the upgraded ones, and the wifi is not great.
Still the best place to stay if you dont mind being a little bit outside Jindy.

Well, where do I even begin? We booked a cabin 5 months in advance and specifically requested one next to the lake. We even spoke directly to reception, and they confirmed the request. They also asked us to include the request in the notes for “extra assurance,” which we did, of course.

Fast forward to two days before check-in, and we get a call saying they "missed" our request and now couldn’t provide us with anything near the lake. But don't worry, they had ONE cabin available - right next to the road! How’s that for a perfect lake getaway? The cabin was nowhere near the peaceful lakeside we had envisioned, and instead, we got the delightful sound of traffic as a bonus.

They offered a refund (how considerate), but let’s be real - two days before travel during peak school holidays, there’s no way we could find a last-minute replacement. It's honestly baffling how this was handled. We booked well in advance, confirmed everything, and still ended up with a total mess. Definitely not the stress-free experience we were hoping for.
Fantastic staff who were really helpful and keen to ensure our stay was great. Facilities were clean and warm.
The only complaint was that the spa and games room both weren't working. The rooms were beautiful and the staff were really kind. Lakeside too!

The cabin looked nice at first, but upon closer inspection we could see that half of the beds were still dirty, the towels had suspicious brown stains on them, there was chewed gum on the tv, the fan was not working, and worst of all the tap water would sometimes be murky and at times be brown! A lot of things were out of order including the spa and games room and we didn’t have many options for things to do here at this park.
Great park but very tight for positioning vans, clean amenities and best of all right on the lake. Very friendly staff.

Lovely caravan park in Jindabyne. The cabin we were in was great - decent size with storage for 4 bikes (just) and more than ample space for a long car. The place was clean and well stocked in terms of cooking and eating gear. Beds were comfortable.

Heating and cooling were adequate although the rooms in the cabins do not have separate heating/cooling - just a fan each.

Communal areas looked good. I can imagine it gets packed during winter and could do with more facilities, I think.

However the paths between sites and cabins are on the narrow side, just wide enough for one car, making it interesting when you get a car coming the other way or needing to turn a big vehicle or towing vehicle around.

We did not have much to do with the staff, having arrived late. So not sure what service is like. We did notice that no dishwashing powder/tablets were provided and just two rubbish bags for a 7 day booking for 6 people. But I’m sure this would have been addressed had we asked them instead of relying on ourselves.

The park is located on the lake, with access to a swimming beach, near a boat ramp and a hot place for water sports. It is a 5 minute drive or 10 minutes on a reasonably flat bike path to the centre of Jindabyne. There is a cafe and a restaurant right at the front gate.

Although there is some kids playground equipment, our kids were disappointed and felt this could be improved. But there were plenty of activities in the area to keep them entertained.

Wifi was hit and miss and casting to the TV did not work. Remotes for a TV and a fan were missing.
I have stayed at every caravan park in Jindabyne and this is far the best! Friendly staff clean rooms great value would definitely recommend this place.

This is a very bad hotel experience, no doubt about it. It can be said to be the worst experience in my life. The hotel management said that the room smelled of cigarettes because I smoked a cigarette outside the door. This is impossible. They said that the whole room needs to be cleaned and a fine of $500 is required. This is incomprehensible. I am 700km away from this hotel. I was notified the next day after checking out. I will never let anyone around me go to this hotel again.

My kids and I stayed here as a base for a few days in the mountains. The cabin was clean, comfy and equipped well for us to self cater breaky and lunches. The staff were excellent; we walked down to the lake to swim and rent a cat to sail around on. A very short drive into town, or a ride along a cool track. Kids want to head back asap!!
Nice place to base yourself for the National Park. Good sites facing the lake and good amenities.

Discovery Parks Jindabyne is a very clean, well appointed and well positioned park about a 20 minute drive from the SkiTube.  The park overlooks the lake which has a concrete path you can walk or use to cycle into Jindabyne.  We stayed in a deluxe 2 bedroom cabin which had a good sized verandah where you can sit outside and view the lake, full sized and well equipped kitchen, separate loungeroom, nice bathroom with a very large shower and 2 bedrooms, one with a queen sized bed and the other catering for 4 kids. Very comfortable, well maintained, clean and tidy.  The staff at reception are very professional and friendly.  We really enjoyed our 3 night stay at the Park and would highly recommend coming here.  We are repeat visitors due to its cleanliness and lovely location on the Lake.  Also caters for campers, caravans and campers.  Highly recommend a stay.
Clean comfy bed. Good size.
Great place to stay just out of Jindabyne but that makes it a lot quieter of a night time.
Nice hot tub and sauna
Super polite and friendly staff, complimentary kit kats, bottle water and welcome note in cabin on arrival. Fantastic prices, clean well appointed cabin, highly recommend staying here.
来雪山玩最好的房车营地。
Excellent park. Super clean and spacious cabin (we booked the delux) with fully equipped kitchen, everything you need.  Friendly staff.  Best place to stay in Jindy.
They provide pretty much all you need but the cleaning was not deep enough.
Great cabins, perfect location for skiing without the on-snow price tag
Accommodation was very good with all amenities and well maintained. Recommended place to stay with family ☺️

This park is better than the other main park in Jindabyne. We stayed at both in the same week, as we had to leave Jindabyne for a couple days. This time we decided to price check only to find the discovery park was roughly 25% cheaper for the same thing. Therefore we gave it a go. To our suprise, the discovery had much better facilities and things for the kids to do in the park. We'll be certainly staying there again next time we hit the slopes.
Very Happy Customer

The team at Discovery park Jindabyne is beyond legendary. We happened to stay there during the recent strongest wind storm with our tent which got destroyed by the wind while we were up at snow. The office rang notifying us about the damage. We had no option but to pack the stuff away and look for a new place. Luckily the team was there to help, did some juggling and adjustments to other bookings and managed to put us in a cabin for the next two nights. Couldn't be happier to pay a little extra and take on the opportunity to jump from site to site. Kids love this place, so do we. Convenience is everywhere, play equipment, great cozy amenities, a fireplace, hot sauna, clean bathrooms. Great in summer as it's right on the lake. And it's in the perfect mid location between snow and the town. From now on only staying at this park while in the mountains. Biggest thanks to Crystal, Trent and the rest of the team who kindly looked after us.
Love it fuel station next door and thai food next door too front office has 25% off ski hire card
Clean & tidy site. Comfy rooms. Helpful staff.

Beautiful modern cottage with everything we needed, located 25 min away from the slopes. We really enjoyed our stay (family of four). Comfy beds too! The only downside is the quality of tap water, there's so much chlorine, and boiling doesn't help. I had to fill my water bottles at Friday Flat Bar.

They upgraded us to a deluxe cabin free of charge (amazing), but there were several issues with it. I would not have been happy to pay extra for this cabin. Firstly, the key they gave us was bent, though we managed to bend it back into shape. The exit boom gate was stuck open due to damage from a previous visitor. When we got to our cabin, the door was very sticky because the tracks were poorly maintained. Several dishes in the cabin were filthy, one of the oil heaters was broken, and the toilet extraction fan made a terrible noise.

Other visitors informed us that we were expected to throw out our rubbish, but we had no idea where the park's garbage disposal was located and weren't told this was required. It seems the only cleaning done is a sweep, replenishment of soaps, and a brief check-over and replenish clean sheets and towels. I understand they have a lot to manage, so I can forgive them for overlooking a few things in our cabin. However, the spa was broken the entire time, and three of the games in the game room were also broken, despite guests having to pay to use them.

The staff were very polite but seemed very busy and understaffed, so we can forgive many of the issues. I hope they address the problems I’ve listed and improve the overall maintenance of the cabins and the park in general, as well as clearly explain the garbage disposal policy.
Beautiful views

Perfect for the winter and found it to be the perfect base for sking at Perisher or Thredbo. It's located just outside of Jindabyne, so you miss the morning traffic and can get to the slopes quickly.

The cabins are reasonably priced and very comfortable, with plenty of warmth and hot water - exactly what you need after a day on the slopes.

I would definitely recommend this park to anyone looking for a convenient and affordable place to stay while skiing in the Jindabyne area.

2nd year in our pop up camper on a powered caravan site and loving the
professionalism and hospitality of the discovery park team

Enjoyed 2 years running in a powered van site!
Toilets, showers are very clean and hot!
Park is also very clean.
Staff very friendly and accommodating!
Definately coming back!
Okay

The bunk beds are very unstable, the tv didn’t work, disappointed in the lack of their supplies such as kitchen , door handle broken. Other than that nice stay.  ||Thank for the stay in number 2 cabin, my friends came and had car service nice time , simple easy access to the road to Thredbo.
Amazing experience.. would definitely recommend others on staying there …. Love the cabin so much.. the cleanliness especially…

Excellent place to stay overall, first trip to the snow and was very short drive to Bullocks flats where we got on the skiube to perisher. Staff very accomadating, great for family's.  Right on the lake Jindabyne.

Great location for a snow holiday, for a family of 5 during snow season the rooms can be a bit tight but are comfortable. The common area has a laundry with multiple washers and dryers and a sitting room with a fireplace for the cold nights, also has a sauna after a long day on the slopes. Woolworths is a 5 minute drive down the road and 30 minute drive to the slopes.

We didn’t stay in the park but stopped at the petrol station to get fuel, change the gas bottle, and fill up the water tank in the caravan.
A lady from Discovery Park saw us taking the gas bottle down from the caravan and waited until we took it to the office. She then informed us that they don't exchange gas bottles there and that no one in town does. When we asked for help on where we could go, she insisted that there was no petrol station does it in town we need to go to hardware store.

Next, we parked the caravan to fill up the water tank at the same tap we used 5 days ago. Again, she saw the entire process while chatting with a colleague. When we were ready to use the hose, she came over and said it wasn't allowed either. We explained that we had asked at the Discovery office 5 days ago and were told it was okay to use that tap. She explained that the staff we spoke to before were new and didn’t know.

I then asked if we could pay for a day in Discovery Park to use the water and how much it would cost. She said she didn’t know and didn’t even offer to find out. The moral of the story is that she works at a service station/Discovery Park office where she is supposed to help people, but she was not only unhelpful but also seemed uninterested in assisting us. She lacked the necessary information and could have at least been polite in saying she didn’t know.

We are a regular discovery park customers and never had this bad experience.

By the way, at the town petrol station, (where they were super friendly)you can swap gas bottles and fill up water at the caravan park for $5, which goes to charity(and were super helpful and friendly too)

Great accom for our ski trip! The cabins have everything you need and are toasty and warm to come home to in the evenings after a day in the snow. Also lovely people, we made a mistake with our booking and they were so understanding and really looked after us. Thank you.

Totally disappointed as we paid $70 a night for a powered sight ($30 in off peak season) only to be told when we got there the spas and saunas would not be working for the length of our stay. All they could do was offer us 10% of our next stay with them, what a joke.
Close to town, and a great location. Accommodation was really nice, stayed in a cabin and there was a good account of room.

Scenic Serenity and Comfort at Discovery Parks - Jindabyne

Nestled by the tranquil waters of Lake Jindabyne, Discovery Parks - Jindabyne offers a picturesque retreat for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike. The park’s location is nothing short of idyllic, providing guests with breathtaking views and easy access to the surrounding natural beauty.

Fully-Equipped Facilities: The park boasts a range of facilities that cater to all needs. From the well-maintained amenities to the variety of accommodation options, guests can enjoy a comfortable stay without any hassle.

Pristine Cleanliness: Cleanliness is a top priority at Discovery Parks - Jindabyne. The rooms and common areas are kept spotless, ensuring a hygienic and inviting environment for all visitors.

Practical Comfort: Practicality blends with comfort here. The accommodations are designed to maximize space and provide all the necessary comforts of home, making it an ideal choice for families, couples, and solo travelers.

Friendly Staff: The warm and welcoming staff at Discovery Parks - Jindabyne go above and beyond to ensure guests have a memorable stay. Their friendliness and attentiveness add a personal touch to the experience.

Overall, Discovery Parks - Jindabyne is a fantastic choice for those looking to immerse themselves in the serenity of the Snowy Mountains region while enjoying all the comforts of a well-equipped holiday park.

We had a fantastic stay at Discovery Park over the Anzac Day weekend! Our cabin's lakeside location was stunning, and the accommodation itself was immaculately maintained and spacious. The nearby Thai restaurant and cafe added to the convenience of our stay. The staff were friendly and efficient, ensuring the park was spotless. We also enjoyed the excellent facilities, including the spa and sauna. The park's proximity to Thredbo, just a 30-minute drive away, made it an ideal base for our adventures. Overall, we thoroughly enjoyed our time at Discovery Park and would highly recommend it!

Clean cabin.
good location.
Great park great location.   Great amenities.  Only let down was only one amenity block. And it was the opposite end to us.

Stayed here with a friend for the weekend. Just wanted somewhere cheap to rest our heads from the big day of hiking. And cheap this was. I'm not sure if we ended up staying in a level above the economy room that i booked, but my friend and I were pleasantly surprised at the size of our accom. We had a queen bed and a bunk bed. I thought we were going to have to share toilet facilities with the rest of the grounds but we got our own bathroom and kitchen facilities so we could comfortably make our own food instead of needing to go out and eat. Despite the accommodation looking dated (not modern like the luxury cabins), they were really clean and well kept. Almost like they were less than a yr old from first installations.
Staff were friendly and it was in a noce location to suit our needs. Would gladly stay again. Thanks guys 😊
Economical, friendly stay and great Thai restaurant attached. Some repairs needed in the cabin.

Great staying!
My girlfriend and I were very impressed with the quality of the cabin we stayed in, everything seemed brand new, clean and organised. Shower was strong and great.
The fireplace was a plus! Kept us warm in the evenings!

The facilities are great, spa, sauna, games room… so much fun. And the site is right by the lake!

Location is incredible. 3 mins drive into town. But you also have a petrol station, cafe and a restaurant right outside the park.

Highly recommend it!

The amenities are good, spa and sauna, hot shower, kitchen, game room and laundry. But the campground is BAD, it’s full of dirty extremely hard ground, had to use a hammer to put the pegs in, it’s super dirty as well because of the dirty condition, the dirty goes into the tent. I know it’s because of the flood the grass was gone, but they should have put the grass first before opening the camping area.

Had a terrific stay at the Discovery Park Jindabyne, well maintained park for campers, caravans and cottages of different sizes.|Beautiful location opposite the Jindabyne Lake.|We stayed in one of the newly built Superior 2 Bedroom Cabin with Fireplace. We had Cabin 90 that is located closest to the Lake.|Extremely comfortable and everything new.|Friendly service on arrival and departure from all the staff at Reception.|As the cabin is self contained very limited supplies of coffee, sugar, tea, S&P are supplied.|Bring your own and don't forget the cooking oil for those stay in nights of BBQ's.|Jumping pillow, chess set, and canoe/kayak hire onsite for further exploring Lake Jindabyne.|Highly recommend a stay here.
Close to Thredbo and easy to go the chairlift. The park was next to a lake so it was a pretty good place to chill.
Not close to town FYI. Rooms are ok but bunks small. No staff after 6 so your key will be in a safe with everyone else's. Pretty good value in summer. Great access to lake.
We stayed in a Superior Cabin.
Stayed for 2 days ...powered site was great...we were in a roof top tent.

I am very disappointed with the service, I made a booking for work related reasons and the very next day my work situation had changed which was out of my control. So I called them the next day and asked for a refund and they would not out of goodwill refund me the full amount and charged me 1 night stay. They offered to reschedule but I wasn't travelling for a holiday so I wasn't able to choose that option. So please be careful before booking and keep this in mind because they will show no mercy or goodwill if you need to make a cancellation for any reason and will only act upon their policy.

Our site was  awful
Sand gravel rocks we were at the back of the caravan
Park where their were old cars boats
And to walk to the bbq site up again gravel
Rocks plenty of other sites that had cement sites  sad
Great location had a lovely time
Great location. Good push-bike track. Even though it's a massive caravan park, I'm surprised how quiet it is.  Right in the lake.

We had a family stay for my husbands 60th birthday.  We all would highly recommend staying here. Really enjoyed all the facilities.  Great playground and jumping pillow for our grandson.  Cabins were extremely clean. Staff were always helpful and friendly. We definitely will be returning for a longer stay.
This is our go to stay whenever we are passing through or spending time in Jindabyne. Prices are excellent, service is always exceptional and rooms are always clean and comfortable.

Discovery Parks Kakgoorlie and Bubury WA. |I took up membership at Kakgoorlie Discovery Park last year. 2023. |On Sunday 25th Feb 24 I book online. With my Member Number. |I arrived at Bunbury Discovery Park to take up.my site. In which I have never been to before. |Staff Member claims My Name & Member Number has been Blacklisted? |I am shocked, dismayed and livid all in one. |She also claims to have returned My Online Payment. |Here's hoping it returns to my bank account. |Worst caravan Park Organisation Nationally I've ever !

very comfortable！
Recommend everyone to stay here.

It was my first time visiting Jindabyne.

The location of discovery park is superb, you can easily turn up to Kosciusko road after you exit the park. We booked the deluxe cabin with lake view, has everything we need. The bedroom is a bit small though. The living room is equipped with AC so good for hot days. Although I suspect in winter whether it will be cold in bedroom.

Overall, good value for money considering the average airbnb price is up now.

The powered sites are extremely small and poorly laid out. I tow a 12 foot van and had difficulty fitting into a 21 foot site. Far from Mt fav caravan park. The wb sit was difficult to navigate and it is impossible to ring for info in advance.

We stayed a night in summer in a cabin on our way out of a bigger camping trip. The park ticked all the boxes we needed for great value and great facilities- we used the sauna and spa; kids had a bit of a jump on the jumping pillow and playground; and the Maya Thai restaurant was excellent. Cabin was clean and cosy with a great spa bath. The communal living room and kitchen looked like great spaces, though we didn’t use them.

Cabin was cozy space wise, but advertised to show this. Staff were friendly, we always saw maintenance/gardening staff about. Facilities were incredible. Easy access to the water & cycle path in to Jindabyne.

This was a great return trip to the park, which had been noticeable uplifted since our last visit. We stayed in a deluxe cabin which was spacious and clean.  The balcony was very relaxing.||The grounds were well maintained and I was surprised at how clean and nice looking the amenities block was.  It’s great to see the Maya Asian restaurant and Trader Joe’s cafe on-site too - they really supplement the park.||We flagged a couple of minor maintenance things with reception who flagged with maintenance to fix.||Highly recommends
Not bad cabins.   But book a superior.   Park is a bit over developed, cabins packed very tightly together.   But pretty good overall.
Well priced and great views
Amazing hotel with cheap cabin prices and a lot of recreational activities. The location is also pretty good being right next to Lake Jindabyne.

Pros
Large amenity block with lots of toilets and showers so no need to wait on one
Spa facilities and sauna
Large kitchen and lounge area
Gorgeous views of Lake Jindabyne
Nice little grass plot with a driveway for car
Nice ladies on reception
Great location for thredbo and charlottes pass roads

Cons
Full of drunk adults who let kids run wild
No curfew like other parks so noisy all night with no security checking
Sign said no kids in spa, walked in to 15 under 10s splashing in the spa like a pool with the adults just chatting in the other one
Thoroughfare for cyclists and runners from outside so something to keep in mind
It is a bit far from town so would have to drive in to shops etc or walk 35 minutes

We 2 families were staying in Discovery Park Jindabyne from 23 Dec to 28 Dec for 5 nights. Gerry is very helpful and friendly to answer my questions as we were here for the first time. Our cabins got  upgraded upon our arrivals on 23rd and it is an awesome presents for our 2023 Christmas. We are so grateful. The location is great! We definitely love to come back and bring more my friends families here. Thank you very much for your kindness and servies, Discovery Park Jindabyne!
Perfect location, close to city centre and right next to the lake track. Cottage are nice and cozy.

We went to hike Mt kosciuszko and booked a cabin for one night. The cabin made use of its space having all the ammenties we needed such as a kitchen, toilet, Tv and 2 rooms with a queen and bunk beds. The room was clean and had things for you to use such as plates, cutlery, blankets and towels. The airconditioner also worked amazingly. They gave us complimentary chocolates, hot cocoa mix , water bottles and tea and coffee sachets. They also gave my kids a booklet to complete and colouring pencils which were amazing as it provided a solution to boredom on the long drive home. The only let down was the games room. My son wanted to play some games but the note to coins exchanger was broken and the only game working was one that was so confusing for my daughter and son.

The two bedroom cabin was quite spacious.  The second bedroom with the bunk beds was very small. The beds were comfortable.

On the flip side, they didn't provide enough supplies for a family of 5, for a multinational night stay. The location is about 4 km from the main road.

Great location for close to Jindabyne township and with restaurants and fuel part of the same complex.  A tidy and clean grounds and well laid out.  The amenities building was a wonderful surprise with 2 big sitting rooms, games room, big laundry, toilets/shower and a spa/sauna room.  Friendly, helpful staff.
This Discovery Park is the Best we have stayed at, And we have been to plenty of Discovery Parks all over Australia. Definitely 5 Stars
Absolutely fantastic stay at this park. Very helpful staff, and great facilities 👍
Absolutely amazing location. The cabin was modern and very comfortable. The team where super friendly. Definitely will be returning.

A mate pre-paid 5 nights in a 2 bedroom (1 queen room and 1 bunk room), I joined him 2 nights into his stay. I pre arranged with reception my arrival. At checkin i had pretty much beg for a 2nd key and i was told i couldn't have a boom gate pass. This did my head in as the park was far from full, so they would of had plenty.  I offered to pay extra and i was still told no.  Other than that, no complaints the cabin was great.
Best facility. Very satisfied by efficient service. Cleanses and cabin ambience is top notch
Absolutely amazing location. Peaceful, well modernised and comfortable. Cant recommend the service and location enough!

Run down facilties but clean.
Newer cabins are available with fireplaces.
Decent water pressure in showers.
Handy restaurants on site.
Activity equipment is available on loan. Fishing rods, tennis racquets, and balls.
Jumping pillow and chess nice touch for the kids.
There are lots of picnic tables, bbq plates, and bins throughout the park.
Lake access.
Extremely accommodating. The staff are very friendly and welcoming. They even went out of their way to help us in a bind.  The facilities are great and the location is perfect. I'll be back.

Ever felt so lost and cold, then someone lends you a helping hand?

My wife and I visited the site this June during the snow season. We stayed at the powered tent site. For a day or 2 , the gust was blowing close to 100km/h and our tent and gazebo were fluttering throughout.

After a day of snowboarding up the mountain, we came back in the evening to our site in total darkness, the tent had collapsed inwards with total structural failure, and the gazebo blown a few meters away still hanging onto a few remaining pegs on the ground. The whole site was a disaster. Neighbouring tents were in similar situation. Wind was still blowing and it started to rain.

We initially called the emergency helpline, but no one was picking up. So we drove around hoping to find the person that was doing the rounds. Luckily we found him and he was able to set us up in one of the available cabin that same night.

The next morning the manager called to check if we were ok, and was kind enough to let us stay in the same cabin for the rest of the stay.

We truly felt grateful in this unfortunate event and appreciate the kindness of the staff of Discovery Park Jindabyne.

In terms of the park, it has a great ammenities block. Tons of toilets and showering cubicles, great hot water and pressure. Has a sauna room too!

It also has tv room, commercial kitchen and place to eat within the ammenities block all heated during winter.

The cabin we stayed in was a little aged, but has all the things you'd require, such as kitchenette, a/c, little table and sofa. The one we stayed in didn't have an ensuite.

I highly recommend the park and the staff! We will definitely be back! :)

Can't wait to go back!!!
Best trout fishing!!
Affordable and comfortable cabins.
Everything at your fingertips.
10/10

Very poor value for money....rooms plain and under prepared..fridge door left ajar..heater left on ...room staff shpukd have corrected all of tjis and failed to make up one bed...cramped and shower stained with red mould. very expensive for what's on offer and what it is. Staff are blunt and to the point but not rude... just not welcoming perhaps?...dunno...it's like it's an inconvenience turning up with a boat and trailer  to them. Very hard to navigate internal roads....it's cluttered...over developed and cramped....probably the poorest and most overpriced park I've stated in frankly....
Comfy, clean & friendly in a great spot👍
Staff were very friendly. Park amenities are outstanding.

Expensive, but what isn't in Jindabyne.
The rooms were more than we expected.  The amenities are good, but the showers and toilets could do with an update.
Really nice and convenient

Convenient location & reasonable price. Room grills & some elements don't work on stoves. Communal oven doesn't work. Aircon/heating in rooms good. Beds comfortable. Showers have good pressure. Bath runs fast for a muscle easing soak. Games room machines eat coins.

Our cabin was a bit small and dated but it was clean and well appointed. We had all the essentials for our trip to the snow: a hot shower, efficient air conditioning and a comfortable bed. Staff were welcoming and informative. There’s a nice Asian restaurant just outside the entrance and the park is conveniently located for the drive to the ski fields.  Recommend the walk along the lake into town.
Awesome place! Perfect for trips with family and friends. Don't forget to try the big bouncy thing in the park.

This place is amazing from booking to staying. The lady I spoke with on the phone was so lovely and helped me choose the most suitable cabin for my family.  We stayed at one of the new cabins with a fireplace. It was beautiful and clean with all the modern facilities  with a small Kitchen. We actually cooked dinner at the cabin twice during our 5 night stay and everything was perfect. We think the price is very reasonable for August which is right in the middle of the ski season. I highly recommend this place and we will definitely be returning.
Perfect for a place to sleep
Location is prime,

We stayed at Jindabyne Discovery Park in early July. The accommodation and staff were lovely and better than other similar holiday parks we have stayed before. Unfortunately, in one early morning during our stay, strong gusty wind blew down large branches from a nearby tree causing damages to our car. Following our stay, the management team promptly attended to and addressed the issue with a satisfactory outcome, which we really appreciated. Kudos to Gerry, Jeff and your team for being professional and highly customer-focused. We highly recommend other travelers to Jindabyne to stay at this park.

Nice gem. Air conditioning unit is in a weird spot in the hallway. Doesn’t help when it’s -10 degrees and you can’t warm up the cabin properly. Facilities around good. Service decent. Beds uncomfortable.

Great basic and extra amenities provided in the cabin and surrounding area. Just take note if you are working remotely like me and need access to the wifi or your own data just hope and pray it will work throughout because I got cut from the wifi numerous times and cell service dropped to 1 bar. So perhaps a better reception and wifi service won't hurt after paying a fair amount 🤷‍♂️

Usually, holiday park bed & mattress was hard & thin, but here, I enjoy resting my whole body in comfort soft & thick in my queen bed after long hours playing in the snow. Maybe the blanket was not that thick but that not a problem. The heater works perfectly.I sleep like a baby thank you 🥰

Seriously dissatisfied with standard room. I went during July school holidays weekend

Location wise it’s excellent, 18kms from bullock flat and 30 kms from Perisher. Gas station right outside. There is an cafe and Thai restaurant next to reception. Car parking was next to the door. I liked the lake inside the premise. Amenities games are paid ones, didn’t try. There is a tennis court and children play area. Overall atmosphere was okay

Problem is quality of the room. My bathroom door was not closing properly, I had to push every time. Hot and cold water mix is outdated model , taps are separated, you are lucky to get right mix. Bedspreads were clean but new blankets had human hair. Had kitchen with stove but cramped. Floor found with chocolate wrappers when we entered. AC was okay to manage the heat. I had issue with entrance door lock and staff arrived quickly though it’s after office hours, I appreciate that

I got it for $1700 for 3 days. Maybe I got it very cheap not sure but my concern is cleanliness. I didn’t complain during my stay as I was busy and arrived after office hours

I will not book again with discovery parks, I can’t recommend standard rooms to anyone. Other type of rooms , I am not sure
Best aspect was the friendly and very helpful staff. Depending on when you book, it can be cheaper than nearby hotels even in your own cabin. Great children's play area. free activities like tennis.

We stayed for only night one sadly … we ended up at an awful hotel on the other side of Jindabyne for the next three nights and were wishing we had known about this accomodation earlier to extend our booking. For what it is this accommodation is wonderful. Great location for trips to Perisher-smiggins. So clean, warm and bright. To our great fortune I think we may have been upgraded. We stayed in room 55 which was lovely.  I thought the bunk bed was fine to sleep on. This is not a luxurious 4.5-5 star hotel experience but a clean, modern, warm, bright and homely cabin. We had a kitchenette and a bathroom in room 55. Great price and great value. I dreamt about being back here for the next 3 days lol. We received a welcoming email/  text and warm return call to arrange our late arrival. Thank you so much for a beautiful cabin and beautiful service. We appreciate it.

Very Very convenient location if you are going to Perisher.
There is also a Petrol station and a restaurant so you can refuel.
It is only an half hour from Perisher so if you want the best parking location then you can start around 6:30am. I you just want to park at Perisher then you can start latest at 7:15am to make it to the ski resort parking area.

The cabin is just alright. It has air conditioner which is sufficient to keep you toasty, there is a tv as well.

There is wifi but no guarantee you xan get connected and the speed is not great so use it if you really have no option.

Would I recommend? surely yes.

Lovely location, the receptionist were super lovely and the cabins super cute! We stayed in cabin 37, nice heater, clean sheets, clean cabin but the bunk beds are so uncomfortable. The mattresses definitely need replacing as they are super thin and I can feel the slats underneath. The double bed however is nice and comfy. Amenities are quite nice, plenty of showers and toilets and 2 huge spas. Accomodation is a great price definitely worth it as you can spend thousands trying to secure something this close to the snow during winter! Will come back.

The front staff were lovely. The staff greeted us with a smile and explained the amenities and where we would stay.

We stayed in a 2-room cabin for a night. The beds are nice and cozy, the shower is good and hot, the A/C heater is excellent, everything is clean, and essential kitchen equipment is provided. Unfortunately, we have to put our bunk bed sheets ourselves.

They have a spa, game centre, shared kitchen, and fireplace open until 9 pm or 10 pm. You also have a nice view of the lake. The location is very convenient, 5 mins drive to Jindabyne centre, less than 30 mins drive to Perisher or Thredbo. The cabin is also the least expensive 2-room cabin we found in the area.

So far, we have liked our stay here. Hopefully, more than a night next time.

The park is great and when in Jindabyne will always stay here until now.

Paved powered site with camping trailer. Cracked pipe from site behind meant waste seeped through retaining wall and into our site where we sat.

About 8:30pm on 1/7/23 parked vehicle horizontal to trailer due to flood from site behind. Vehicle was not causing any obstruction to person/vehicle. Groundsman or whatever low job "Danny" has pulled Mitsubishi triton hard up against site on bend demanding vehicle be moved. "You can't park that there, you need to move" Rude behaviour not appreciated late in evening when kids asleep inside.

Following morning informed reception of issues who were disinterested. Pretended to write on notepad of concerns. Asked management to call me and discuss further with no reply.

Disappointed in the park which was always been of high standard.
Great people willing to go above and beyond 👍🏼 Even to find a wheel brace ;) ;)

Cozy stopover amid chilled air.
Loved it!!!
Great location very clean cabin and great atmosphere diffidently come again🙂

This park is definitely NOT family orientated. If you coming with kids under 14 yr of age, you won’t be allowed to use spa and sauna with kids after a good day skiing.
All guests are being kept asked by random staff if they are staying in resort and allowed to use the facilities. Get F…. We pay money to come and enjoy great family time without being harassed. If you have management problems or have people entering the resort from street to use the facilities, deal with it some other way to let your guests enjoy their stay.
Great little spot for a day or 2 stopover, very clean and even better pricing
